title: Runtime Sanitizers, Dynamic Analysis, and Language Profiles · Gate Lens: Ubsan
summary: Add runtime instrumentation to native and managed-language test profiles. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates ubsan increment in a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer with appropriate recover or trap policy.
Use AddressSanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Ubsan evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-ubsan-qrrcw1: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ubsan into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-ubsan-qrrcw1:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Ubsan model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Ubsan applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer with appropriate recover or trap policy.
